name: True Garlic Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the garlic puree and softened butter.
2. Combine the yeast with half a cup of warm water in a large bowl. Stir with a fork or small whisk. Add an additional two and a half cups water, salt, and flour (one cup at a time), beginning with whole wheat flour. Use a whisk until stiff, then switch to a wooden spoon.
3. Turn the dough onto a well-floured work surface. Knead rhythmically for 10 to 15 minutes until smooth, springy, non-sticky, and elastic. Add more flour as needed while kneading.
4. The dough is ready when indentations from your fingers spring back. Cover with a cloth and let rest while you prepare the bowl.
5. Clean and dry the bowl, then generously butter it. Place the dough in the bowl, turning to coat with butter. Cover and place in a warm, draft-free area until doubled in bulk (about 1 1/2 hours). It is ready when gently poked; the hole remains.
6. Dust baking sheets liberally with cornmeal. Divide the dough into three equal parts. Keep unworked portions covered. Flour your work surface.
7. Roll each piece of dough into a rectangle approximately 14 inches long by 7 inches wide using a rolling pin.
8. Spread softened garlic butter over the dough, then roll from one long edge to the opposite, as if rolling up a rug. Pinch ends closed. Place on sheets.
9. Slash loaves at two-inch intervals with a sharp knife or razor blade.
10. Cover and place in a warm, draft-free area until doubled (about 1/2 hour). Meanwhile preheat oven to 400°F.
11. Bake for 35-40 minutes with a pan of boiling water on the oven floor. Spray loaves with water several times during baking to encourage a thick crusty shell.
12. Test doneness by lightly tapping the loaf; it should sound hollow. Serve warm or at room temperature, cooled on wire racks.
